data.title: Write RED structural tests for Part C in test-epic-scrutiny-pipeline.sh
data.description:
## Testing Mode
UPDATE

## TDD Requirement
Add 3 test functions to tests/skills/test-epic-scrutiny-pipeline.sh that FAIL (RED) before Part C is added to epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:
- test_pipeline_contains_part_c_section: grep -q "^### Part C" "$PIPELINE_MD"
- test_pipeline_part_c_trigger_condition: grep -q "2+ other files" "$PIPELINE_MD"
- test_pipeline_part_c_output_format: grep -q "covered_by_SC" "$PIPELINE_MD"

Exemption: Rule 5 / static assets — non-executable LLM instruction file; structural boundary tests only. Behavioral correctness assertions are prohibited.

## Implementation Steps
1. Open tests/skills/test-epic-scrutiny-pipeline.sh
2. Add test_pipeline_contains_part_c_section() after test_pipeline_contains_fidelity_review_with_feasibility()
3. Add test_pipeline_part_c_trigger_condition()
4. Add test_pipeline_part_c_output_format()
5. Add calls to all 3 functions in the "Run all tests" section
6. Confirm bash tests/skills/test-epic-scrutiny-pipeline.sh exits non-zero (RED)

## File Impact
- tests/skills/test-epic-scrutiny-pipeline.sh: modify (add 3 test functions)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] test_pipeline_contains_part_c_section function exists
  Verify: grep -q "test_pipeline_contains_part_c_section" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/skills/test-epic-scrutiny-pipeline.sh
- [ ] test_pipeline_part_c_trigger_condition greps for literal "2+ other files"
  Verify: grep -q '"2+ other files"' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/skills/test-epic-scrutiny-pipeline.sh
- [ ] test_pipeline_part_c_output_format function exists
  Verify: grep -q "test_pipeline_part_c_output_format" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/skills/test-epic-scrutiny-pipeline.sh
- [ ] RED confirmation: bash test file exits non-zero before Part C is added
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/skills/test-epic-scrutiny-pipeline.sh; [ $? -ne 0 ]
